The earliest Khmer multi-shrine (or “multi-aedicular”) temples consisted of rows of nearly identical prasats, six at Preah Ko (880,) three at Phnom Krom, Phnom Bok and Prasat Bei (all 889-915,) five at Prasat Kravan (921) and nine at Prasat Thom in Koh Ker (928-944.)
